top_rant.title: DIRTY, COVERED WIITH OIL

top_rant.text:
After nearly 10 years, our previous juicer kicked the bucket, although the motor was still solid (no more replacement blades available). We ground it down till it just couldnt chew anymore. For $150 and regular use, that's way more than I thought we'd ever see out of it!

I did my amazonian research (as usual for big purchases) and saw the outstanding reviews on this brand and several models. Being a dual career household with three kids, we don't have time to chop, chop, chop and wait, wait, wait for other top quality juicers like the Omega's or Champions. (despite the added bonus of other features, such as making nut butters and such, and potential for higher quality juice. Something has to give!) Being in a pinch, I found this brand and other comparables are also carried by Bed Bath and Beyond (in-store) for approx the same price, so I drove to the local store to get my hands on a floor model and see the quality for myself before I was about to drop this kind of cash!

QUALITY

I WAS STUNNED at the quality and engineering put into this juicer! (No, not mesmerized by the surface stainless steel features, which can cover any kind of crappy plastic parts.) After inspection and visible/touch sensory investigation were duly completed, it went home with us since we needed one asap.

From out-of-the-box through first use and clean-up, this juicer just blew my expectations completely OFF the charts, and I'm a tough sell - being extremely disappointed with the severe drop in quality of American and top quality foreign brand made products anymore, which you pay big money for (don't get me started). They just don't make things like they used to - but this may be an exception. Read on.

OUT OF THE BOX EXPERIENCE

- Kudos to the packaging designers - the out of the box presentation was exceptional, and not cheaply done, which says something. Points for that, as it was stark enough to catch my attention. (Funny how you don't realize how low your expectations have been taken over time, until you encounter something unexpected that raises the bar substantially.)
- Sturdy box, extra thick and sturdy stryo-encasement. Took my 3 yr old to hold the box while I pulled out the styro-bound unit.

- Right on top was the THICK owners manual, neatly nested in it's own little depression of the styro - not jammed along the side like most things you buy. At first glance, it appeared very well designed (which honestly set my expectations up a few notches, after I immediately opened it and flipped through. Being a writer and designer for web, customer experience and first impressions are clear indicators of how great or little care is put into a products presentation and design, and generally further indicates the true quality of what you're spending hard-earned money on.
- PROFESIONNALLY WRITTEN and DESIGNED clearly by a well qualified team of technical writers (my fellow peeps!!)
What a breath of fresh air this is. NOT CHINGLISH (English translated to chinese, translated back to english). No offense people, hear me. No one can expect perfection under those circumstances, it's a way of cutting corners and a clear indicator to me of lack of care, concern and customer experience value. That's at a management/exec level, not he fault of the translators.) Moving on.
- This booklet is very well designed, and professionally published. Pictures are clear, appropriate and headings, sections and notes are clearly indicated with proper visual spacing for scanability and readability. As opposed to most products you'll purchase these days, this manual blew my socks off and is the kind of exceptional quality I would expect from a publisher writing a software user guide, or a "for dummies" book. Straight-forward and to the point, but sans humor. (that's ok, no points lost!) BIG BIG BIG FRIGGIN POINTS HERE GANG!!! This told me A LOT about this diamond in the rough of appliance world, right out of the box. Literally.

- Everything is properly bagged and preassembled, with clear instructions on EASY-TO-REMOVE stickers and well placed cardboard spacers, that protect against any rattling or damage in shipping. (smart, you guys! That impressed me, not just a wadded up cardboard scrap shoved in as usual, but a printed, die-cut cardboard pad with instructions on it. (Impressive...MOST impressive." - Vader) The stickers removed with absolute ease, no picking or scratching of nails to get them off, no hair dryers or Goo Gone. QUALITY... ATTENTION TO DETAIL... MAJOR POINTS. (there Must have been women involved in this design!.... booo hissss..., love you gentlemen, but for real, mmmkay?)

- The plastic components (pulp chute, catcher, and driver are sturdy and thick, quality harder plastics/polymers unlike other products you pay an arm and a leg for (even my last juicer wasn't this good, though it held up well - but I never had the guts to dishwasher it for that reason!) The instructions say dishwasher safe - I may test that theory on this new beasty.

- The "knife-like triangular blade" at the bottom of the feeder tube holds items steady instead of letting them get spun around while you scramble to get the food pusher in place, trying to avoid particles blasting out in your face. That's never happened to me... *ahem... Excellently engineered - and the the tube is stainless steel along with the blade - no risk of that breaking any time soon! But does require caution when rinsing/cleaning! (especially for kids, and most adults...)

- The locking arm is solid stainless steel, and easy to lock and unlock for an adult or older child, which is a good,safe feature. Our last juicer was a struggle even for me at times to hook two arms over the pulp chute and then pull down to lock (let alone unlocking for cleanup... Good God.) The fit is perfect also with the metal arm neatly nesting and locking onto the polymer pulp chute. So again, the engineering and quality of parts is clearly held to high standards (a close relative is a plastics manufacturing engineer, so I'm all too familiar with parts and molding, engineering design and best of all QA. And uh, NOT so QA...!)

- Pulp catcher basket thingie is plenty large enough to contain the bulk of pulp required to fill the pitcher with juice - and with room still to spare. POINTs from this big family with large volume requirements!

- Juice spout has a custom-fitted black silicone (I think) jobby (that's a technical term) that easily fits over the spout for directing the juice straight into a tall glass whilst minimizing splatter. WOMAN POINTS on that one. A well thought out, handy doodad! And perfectly fits the spout with easy on/off. No man-hands or pliers necessary!

- Blade and juice separator. Talk about easy to take off the unit - unlike my old one that you practically lost a nail and shed some blood trying to wedge your fingers in between the juice separater and the motor unit, because that was the only way to safely remove the metal basket/blade unit (on top) without slicing your finger and using unseemly language. This baby fits sits gently on the motor unit with the blade/basket sliding into the gear-thingie-mabob (that turns it) snuggly but not so bad you can't remove it without a jack hammer, or risk throwing juice everywhere when it finally does release. I've cleaned my cabinets off more times than I care to recount (let alone my hair, kids and the floor). BIG BIG BIG POINTS HERE...
- Woman and fingernail friendly, as well as older-child friendly. Excellent, just excellently done.
- AND for those who struggle to know how to seat the basket properly, there's a large white triangle that you line up with the gear-thingie-mabob, which has your choice of three places you can align. KILLER. Half a shake of the lambs tail, unlike minutes and heated temper with our old one - looks easy enough until you try to get it on the blasted thing!

- The Pitcher. OH... MY... WORD... YOU GUYS ROCK!!! For years now, I've grumbled whilst holding my chefs knife over the 2 quart jar (where we transferred juice from our system of Pyrex glass measuring cups to catch the juice, with full splatter effect), to withhold and separate the foam from the juice while pouring into glasses to serve. When I read this feature online, I determined I had to get my grubbly little paws on a unit at the store and see it for myself.
- (Uh huh...heard that marketing mumbo jumbo before... "it slices, it dices...also makes julienne fries!") I've been sold too many times on gimicks and designs that flat don't work. THIS.... THIS HERE WORKS PEOPLE!!! (unless you overfill like some people who shall remain nameless, and even THEN, only the smallest teaspoon escaped - see below for details!) BIG POINTs engineers!! BIG.
- The pitcher fits perfectly snug against the juicer WITH THE JUICE SPOUT INSIDE - so NO splatter! Even my son ooo'd and aaaah'd over that one!! (WHOA! Cool, Mom!) Plus, the pulp-separating lid also has several large slits to let air out but not the juice splatter. I was skeptical at first, but our first use proved me wrong. And by now you KNOW I had to test the limits to see how full you can get it before it fails! Just sayin. I'm impressed - I positioned an unwitting test subject still in his jamma's (sans shirt) just for this test case. :) Hey, you wanna help Mom, sometimes there's risk involved! Nothin but love son...

TEST SUBJECT VEGGIES AND FRUIT:
- very large beets, which had to be halved or quartered to fit the tube (but as large as possible to provide suitable testworthiness and strain on the new system. (hey, if you claim it, I'm gonna test it!)
- whole celery, with only the bottom removed (as much as I could fit in the tube)
- large apples, some firm and crispy, some older and wrinkly (but still viable test subjects and a challenge to the machine)
- large half a head of purple cabbage, cut to fit the tube
- Long leeves of kale, slightly less than perfectly fresh which are notoriously challenging for juicers (and a good test of the variable speed settings)

- The variable motor speed is AMAZING. As an added bonus, the speeds are not merely labeled as numbers...noooo no. The smarty engineers who thought this baby up put WORDS to it (such as Soft Fruits, Pineapple, Apples) for the rest of us nimwits and no-timers who fail to read the instruction guide in full or at all. Greens are not listed, however, which would have been good to have amongst the fruits and veggie labels. However, the small digital screen does place a rigid character limit. So, one must do some associative reasoning to partner fruits/veggies with similar properties. RIGHT. Moving on. Good feature - POINTs on this one.

-First we disassembled and washed everything quick with soap and warm water. Then dried and reassembled for the test run. Elapsed time, about 10 shakes of a lambs tail....maybe a few more, give or take. You get the picture. My 4 yr old could disassemble and reassemble this with a single demo session. No joke. No confusion.

- Veggies and fruit cut down just small enough to fit down the tube, but as large as would safely fit. Gotta push the claims!

- First up, we loaded in the apples: first the crispies and then the wrinklies, all dried out from the fridge during our vacation. My son volunteered you remember, so he did the feeding and pushing, while I did the managing and oversight. Tough job, but somemommy has to do it. Being a very solid, experienced and reliable juice master of our previous unit, he knew how to control his manly although developing almost 9yr old muscles and not over burden the motor. He did well, but there is a learning curve, as with any new appliance.
- First we tested slow speed, recommended for soft fruits. Hey, to be a good tester, you gotta go against the instructions a bit. Do stupid things, well, not stupid like a wooden spoon in the thing, but you know, test the *ahem* "common challenges of potential users". We love all people - some just require more grace at times than others. Moving on.

- Juicer did not like the crispies (apples) so much on low speed, so we vamped it up while juicing to find optimal speed. Does "Apples" for level 4/5 really mean Apples of all kinds and conditions?? Inquiring minds wanted to know. And it does. POINTS FOR ACCURACY.

- The wrinklies went through at the same speed - the drier the apple, the more difficult to grind. Word to the wise: use those vacation-wrinkled fruits don't toss away money if possible! Just use high speed and put them (and your kids!) out of their misery.

- Celery was stellar at the lower speeds, high speeds are fine too (Git-er-dun!!)
- Beets and cabbage required the high speed - which was surprising on cabbage, I was expecting it to be suited to medium, but it struggled a little, so we gave her some more gas. Beets were expected at high.

- Leafy kale took med/hi speed and we took our time pushing it through so the juicer could really gets it's teeth into it and not just chop and throw like our old juicer with dull blades. Like an old horse with few teeth, you're gonna loose some benefit to the good stuff going in! (where did that come from? right, my redneck side. Moving on!)

Despite growing concern from my innocently unsuspecting cohort standing directly in the line of fire, should the splatter effect come into play, the juice pitcher neared full and foam was a flyin' inside the lid - but it never splattered out on him. POINTS POINT POINTS POINTS!!!
- We did have to stop and pour out about 10 oz to continue getting everything through, but that's not the fault of design. the pitcher is more than adequate for a normal sized family, couple or single persons. (yay, spreadin the love.)

ANTI-FOAM POUR SPOUT PITCHER

Odds were not in favor of this working, compared to past experiences and aforementioned expectations. However, I was happily proved utterly and completely wrong. With the pitcher filled to capacity (foam at the top, and some therefore had creeped into the pour spout area, outside the blocking wall neatly designed into the lid), I began to pour into our glasses to see just how much would actually escape. With due credit to the already escaped foam, I was expecting that portion to pour into the cups. BUT, IT DIDN'T. Only about a teaspoon escaped into the first cup, and MIRACULOUSLY... the rest was sucked back behind the wall as I poured the rest. POINTS, POINTS, POINTS, POINTS POINTS POINTS POINTS!!! CORPORATE MGMT: There is NO WAY that engineer or concept designer was paid enough. They deserve bonuses, and a % of the profits ongoing!! Don't be stingey, pay the people fairly!! (not that I'm assuming you're unfair or stingey or miserly...not judging, just noticing a pattern to corporate America these days.) Moving on!

CLEANUP

About 30 shakes of a lambs tail, give or take (and provided the lamb isn't cranked on caffeine or sugar-laden oats).
- Super simple, very quick and again, something my 4 yr old could do (with supervision. Come on, not going to endanger my child, people - chill! Just sayin- a mother knows her kid's skill level and comprehension at that age - and she could do this!)

- INCLUDED CLEANING BRUSH ACTUALLY WORKS, and better than my little nail brush I bought at WalMart and have used for years. The bristles are large enough they do NOT get stuck in the tiny perforations of the spin basket like a nail brush will. GOOD DESIGN PEOPLE. WOMAN POINTS on this as well - THAT is attention to detail!!
- The head is small enough to easily maneuver around inside and out, and the handle is long enough to keep your hands and nails away from danger.

THIS.... This is one impressed new customer Breville. I intend to report back and update this post as my usage goes on.

Hope this was helpful, folks. Spend the money on this sweet little beauty, because this is one of the first products I've bought in many, many, many years that not only surprised me on every possible level, but far exceeded my expectations (well worn down by cheap junk with fancy labels and advertising). HOPE HAS BEEN RESTORED!!!! (and there was much rejoicing....)
top_rant.title: BEST INVESTMENT you can count on for SOLID RETURNS.  - From a tough sell consumer

top_rant.text:
I absolutely adore how this coffee table looks. It is so elegant and the pullout drawers and slide outs all-around it are a great feature. The legs are also sleek and modern but still comfortable. I went to see this coffee table in person in the Ashley showroom before buying it and I would suggest doing the same because there are some quality issues that may bother you - I decided that I loved the look of the table so much that it wasn't an issue, but you might feel differently - I'll tell you the problems below and you can decide for yourself.

Cons
1) The drawers are super cheap feeling. Mostly particle board and slide mechanism is extremely crappy - half the drawers almost fly out and the other half barely slide at all. The handles are really nice and so is the rest of the table so I am not sure why they didn't put more money into the drawers. It's a pretty expensive table and I am suprised that Ashley furniture built something so beautiful with such crappy drawers. In the end, this was my biggest concern but I loved the look of the table too much to let it bother me. I think I might insert some weights into the back of the drawers and put a lining in them (which is what Ashley furniture should have done). Also, I just received it tonight, but I'm going to have my husband see if the track mechanism can be upgraded or at least fixed so the drawers slide better.

2) The slide out pieces are also cheap feeling. There are wood pieces that hold them in place and one of mine isn't attached, luckily I found it in the shipping materials and I am hoping (fingers crossed) that my husband will be able to re-attach it when he gets home from work. Otherwise that slide will be super crooked.

3) The finish seems like it will get ruined quickly. In fact, I almost refused the delivery because the top of the table I got has an indention under the color coat. It looks like its in the wood, but its clearly a blemish and I was upset at first but the seller yelled at me (no kidding) when I called to report it and with all the trouble I had with their company from the beginning I couldn't bear to think of dealing with them for a return so I decided to deal with it - mostly because they bullied me into it by telling me I'd have to pay for shipping back to them and then shipping to Ashley's and then shipping for the new one to me (I already paid them $75 to have this one shipped). Anyway, that's a whole other complaint (see below). The finish was also weird and uneven looking on the bottom shelf piece - again I told this to the seller immediately and got the run around.

4) There are no floor protectors for the legs? Its weird because there are holes in the legs like it was suppose to come with those furniture caps, but I didn't get any and so all I can say is the table should come with them and I'm suprised it didn't. Even low end furniture comes with those! Again, something I figured I would get at Lowe's so not a deal breaker for me.

Pros:
1) The table is HEAVY! In a good way! The table top is solid and so is the shelf and the legs. Again, the only thing that doesn't seem real are the drawers and the pull-outs, the rest of the table is nice wood.

2) The finish is this gorgeous deep brown, almost black, which I love. If you don't want black furniture then I would find something else because it definitely looks black. I really didn't want anything with a red finish because I have oak wood floors in my living room and the cherry finish just looked so weird next to it. This finish is neutral and looks elegant. I like that it doesn't clash with the other wood finishes in my home.

3) The handles! I love them! They are modern and heavy. Nice accents on this table.

4) The legs! There is something so cool about how the legs are cut, they're not just straight and so it makes them interesting - at least to me anyway!

5) Storage space - even though I'm not a huge fan of the puny drawers they put on this gorgeous table, I am a fan of having drawers all around the coffee table. I wanted a lift top that was smaller, but this works just as well for storage purposes and looks better than the plain lift tops.

6) The pull-outs - again, even though I think they are way too cheaply made for this table and what it costs, I still love the pull-outs for drinks and small plates. I think this will really help preserve the finish on the top (which I think is probably pretty delicate).

7) It was easy to put together - I am a 28 year old small female and I put this together all by myself. And it looks great. The shelf is so heavy though - I did have a hard time with that - and getting it into my living room because it comes as 1 big heavy top with the drawers all in it, 4 legs, and the shelf. The top part is extremely heavy - at least for me!

8) It looks amazing - I am in love with how this looks. The size is fantastic, even a little smaller would have been great, but it is slightly smaller than your average coffee table which is nice.

Whatever you do, do NOT and I repeat do NOT buy from AtHomeMart (also goes by the name Mintra corporation). This was a disaster! They sent me an email to call them to give me delivery instructions before they would release it to the freight company, so I called them asap and they went over all the rules about do not sign for it until you see it is not damaged, once you sign for it then it releases the freight company from responsibility, etc. etc. Okay got it no problem. ... BUT then It didn't arrive on time and when I tried to track it, the freight company said they didn't have any record of it. When I contacted the seller they told me they would work on it and get back with me. Several days later, I hadn't heard anything and so I contacted them, they then told me it appeared the package was "lost" and they were trying to locate it and I would have to wait! Then randomly at 7am today (Friday) while I was getting ready for work the delivery company calls, when I answer, they hung up. I called back and they said the truck was on its way from Orlando and would be at our house between 10am and 2pm! Well thanks for the heads up! Geez! Luckily my husband doesn't go in to work until 12:30p so we asked if they could make it before noon or after 4:30p when I would get home - the guy said no that would cost extra to set up a special delivery and they need a 4 hour window between 10am and 2pm - remember he is calling the same day he wants to deliver it. So my husband calls his boss and gets permission to come in at 2pm. At 4pm, my husband was still waiting and his boss was needless to say not happy so he left to come to work. I was on my way home and low and behold there is the driver in front of my house. Nice! He was pushing the paper in my face telling me to sign - whoa buddy, you are late and cost my husband 4 hours of work! He said they never told us they would be there between 10 am and 2pm! OMG! Seriously??? I told him I received instructions from the seller that I couldn't sign it until I inspected it, the delivery guy told me I couldn't inspect it until I signed for it.... WTF? Twilight zone. So I called the seller and a manager got on the phone who asked to speak with the driver - the driver refused to take the phone saying that he did not know this guy and could only talk to his boss - this went on and on for an hour!!!! Finally the delivery guy's boss lets us open the package, the seller asks to stay on the phone so he can make sure everything is okay (I'm thinking that is nice right? wrong), we open it and the packaging was done nicely, there was a huge indention on 1 side but the box was double boxed and bubble wrapped so the indention didn't hurt the table (thank God!). But the top of the table had an indention in it and the shelf finish was all uneven and looked messed up, so I told the manager (seller), the manager then proceeded to yell at me that obviously that is how the table is suppose to be - I told him I would usually believe that but I actually saw this table in the Ashley furniture showroom and it isn't suppose to be like this, he then told me that I would have to pay all these shipping fees if I didn't accept it and kept yelling about how he is good to his customers (again WTF? you're yelling at me), finally I just got off the phone with psycho manager guy. I was so exhausted at this point with dealing with these people that I just told the delivery guy I would sign for it. He told me to write down what happened and let me make a copy of the signed form. Then he left me with a huge mess on my porch and I realized I had no way of lifting this enormously heavy coffee table into my house! Nice! And the shipping cost me $75 by the way. Anyhow, I kept the table, marred finish and all and here it is in my living room. The good news is that at least I don't have to deal with that seller ever again, and I hope no one else that reads this does either.
top_rant.title: Design is beautiful, quality not so great

top_rant.text:
I found this reçliner very comfortable, easy to assemble and exactly what I've been looking for as a disabled senior on a fixed income.

I think I died and went to heaven when I turned on the heat and vibration features!!

Very easy to learn how the control works.
You can choose between 5 minutes and 25 minutes, in 5 minute increments and it kills everything automatically when the timer expires. This is a great safety feature so you don't have to worry about accidentally leaving it on.

One helpful hint; The controller cord is kind of short. Rather than having it come out the bottom or side of the chair, around the side and up over the armrest, run the cord "up into" the chair between the back and seat cushions so that the control comes through the back right corner of the chair. By doing this, you will gain several inches in length. It's easy to do. Remember what you learned in school: The shortest distance between two points is a straight line!!

The electrical connections are pretty much foolproof. They are color coded and self-evident to understand.

The up/down buttons can start/stop in infinite positions throughout the full range by simply taking your finger off the button.

This is great to custom adjust yourself to your ideal position.

For the price, I think it's very reasonable for your own little slice of heaven on earth!!

Update: I see many reviews that the recliner is too short for some people.

If you are 5'7" or less, this reçliner will be suitable for you. Any taller, I would keep looking.

A properly fitting reçliner for you will allow only your feet, (at your Achilles tendon ie. the back of your heel) to hang off the end of the chair when the footrest is up and you are sitting normally into the chair.
( No slouching!)

Anything more, you need a reçliner designed specifically for big and tall people.

This chair's weight limit is 300lbs. I am 265lbs and the chair lifts and lowers to a full recline smoothly and quietly.

If you are over 275-285lbs, do not get this chair. Look for one with a higher weight limit. Amazon carries them up to a 500lb weight limit.

The "Massage" feature is actually "Vibration stimulation." It actually states VIBRATION in the heading of this advertisment. So pay attention to your consideration for this reçliner!

Don't be disappointed when a masseuse doesn't appear when you turn it on!! I actually find it as advertised and very invigorating and relaxing throughout the day.

The vibration function softly "Buzzes" but nothing annoying and is as I expected. It gets the blood pumping with your morning coffee or can lull you to sleep in the evening.

The recliner arrived in perfect condition and was simple to put the 4 total pieces together. The two arms and back slide-lock into position on the base of the chair.

You will notice an audible click when they lock into place by pushing forcefully down on the parts. Be sure to line up each side of each part together.

In other words, do not slide one side on then try to get other side on. The left and right side of each part will only slide together simultaneously.

If you lack common sense and know it, don't buy this chair! You will never even figure out how to get the 95lb base out of the box quickly, easily and without damaging it or yourself!

Also, I would definitely invest the additional money and buy the furniture protection policy advertised with this reçliner in case you ever have a problem with it.

I got the 5 year plan. The standard one year manufacturer's warranty covers things like defects in workmanship, non-working, broken or missing parts etc.

Products are supposed to function as intended at the time of purchase! If it doesn't, say something, or stop your complaining, it's that simple!

But the separate warranty policy covers everything from soup to nuts!! So don't worry, just be happy!

So I have complete and total peace of mind for the next 5 years!! It was the smart thing to do. Save your boxes in case you ever have to send the reçliner back for servicing!

I also recommend buying a properly fitting cover for your new reçliner. It will guarantee your reçliner will remain in pristine condition for a very long time to come.

This reçliner is considered medium size with a 23 inch wide seat. Remember that if you want to get a properly fitting cover.

So if you and graced with a larger behind, keep on looking for a big and tall reçliner!

My cover has 6 pockets on the right side for all my remotes, cell phone and the controller for the recliner. It too, was a smart investment.

By the way, a good quality cover will not diminish the heating function. There's plenty of heat to be had for an aching back. It even radiates up your back into your shoulders.

Most, if not all the negative reviews are from ANJ customers that were ill-prepared or over-expected in this reçliner's price range.

These types of reçliners are very expensive! Just don't over-expect for $550!!

The ANJ staff was awesome to work with and took care of me completely from the time of ordering and shipping, to the time it was delivered to my door.

They even followed my request to mark each of the two boxes; 1of 2 and 2 of 2, so they would be sure to stay together during shipping!!

I am a very savvy and well informed consumer. If you follow my advice, you won't be disappointed in buying this reçliner!!

Update: 5-4-21

I had an issue with my chair and I have to add a warning to my review.

I thought this chair was built better than it actually is. One day I woke up from taking a nap in this chair, and like probably millions of us do, I stretched my body before bringing the chair upright again, and I heard and felt a pop from the left rear corner where the seat back connects to the base.

I immediately inspected that corner and was very surprised to find that the seat back had broken right above this connection!!

All I did was stretch for God's sake!!

Upon closer inspection I found no reinforcements at this critical stress point. Definitely a bad design flaw.

I contacted ANJ and after trying to explain numerous times what happened, they finally understood once I sent them a video and pictures of the damage.

Long story short, they sent me a new replacement seat back which I was just lucky they still had in stock that matched my chair's fabric.

But what got me angry with customer service is that they apparently held shipping my package for a total of 5 days because, I figured out on my own having been in the warehousing business my whole life, they only ship their products out once a week in order to save themselves money on shipping charges. In other words, bulk shipping rates.

That didn't bother me, I understood that. What pissed me off is they definitely lied to me when they claimed to have sent my shipment out, and when it actually was passed off to FedEx and it began its trip north to me.

I'm only in Michigan, they are in Georgia. We are not THAT far apart!!

Because they held my package for 5 days to save money, then lied about that fact, it took a total of 10 days to reach me!! From Georgia, I'm not in Alaska!!

Had they just been honest with me and said, "Gee, we have missed our cutoff time for outgoing shipments this week, but we will certainly send it out next Wednesday and you should have it by Monday."

Instead, they passed the buck and blamed FedEx and Covid numerous times for the delay.

Once FedEx did actually had possession of my package, it only took 5 days to reach me from Georgia.

Had they been honest and just told me about their weekly shipping policy, I would've been fine with the timeframe.

So beware, they don't make chairs like we do in the USA, and customer service, although basically nice and helpful, don't always tell their customers the full story!!

So what did I learn from this experience??

I'll NEVER stretch in this chair again!!

Jeff Stearns

Whitehall, Michigan

top_rant.text:
So a couple weeks ago I was shopping for a vacuum cleaner and after watching the silly Shark Rotator infomercial (which made the product look like the best thing since sliced bread), I headed to the Internet to get the real, salesman-free scoop on the product. To my surprise, I couldn't find any bad reviews. I even found one glowing review which was kind enough to break down the different variations sold by different outlets (Michael Flux's review here on Amazon). I took the advice in that review and decided to buy at Bed Bath & Beyond.

I went to retailmenot first to get this 20% off coupon that everyone was talking about but there is none. Not only that, but even if it had provided one, the BBnB Web site doesn't offer you the opportunity to put in a promo code when checking out. With that said, here's how to get the "20% off your first purchase" that others have mentioned: 1) you can go to the BBnB Web site and at the very top of the page there is a link for "E-Mail signup" OR 2) you can text the word OFFER to 239663 to sign you up for text alerts. From what I read about the email alerts, it gives you a printable in-store coupon and I was looking to avoid driving out to the store during the holiday season so I chose the text alerts--not to mention the fact that right now there is free shipping on orders over $99 so I would just be paying the item price minus 20% plus tax with no drive to the store. I figured a text alert (as opposed to an e-mailed in-store coupon) would give me some way to apply my 20% off when shopping online even though there didn't appear to be a mechanism for promo codes. It took three days to get my first text alert after signing up. Then, after I got it, the link in the text opened up a Web page that a cashier could scan in-store. So I was a little pissed...It would appear I would have to go to the store anyway. But then I read the fine print and saw that if I enter my mobile number (the one I use to receive my text alerts) when checking out, it would apply my 20% off coupon to my online order. PROBLEM SOLVED; my cell phone number was--in essence--the promo code.

Now, when I started this process, the item was in-stock at BBnB. But by the time I got the promo code 3 days later it was...OUT OF STOCK!!! Damn, this was not going well. Maybe something was telling me not to buy this vacuum. Anyway, when I browsed over to the Web site and saw that it was out of stock, I also saw that the one I placed in my shopping cart a few days back was still in there (usually I'm good about clearing my browser history and cookies, I don't know what happened). Anyway, I figured, let me give checkout a shot. I fully expected at some point during the checkout that it would say, "sorry, this item is out of stock." It didn't. Then after ordering I expected an email within a day or two saying, "sorry this item is out of stock." But the only thing I got in a day or two was my vacuum! Yes, the vacuum came within two days. I ordered at 2AM on Sunday night/Monday morning. As I write this, it's Tuesday, 5PM and I already got it! Wow, I was expecting two weeks or so but I guess technically it was one business day later.

The first thing I noticed was that the parts did not seem as "solid" as everyone led me to believe it would be. The last vacuum I bought (which still works great but is the one I'm replacing) was a Eureka "The Boss". I remember when I got it about ten years ago, it didn't seem all that solid compared to the metal vacuum I had before it. But the performance of "The Boss" compared to the old vacuum was worth losing some durability. As it turned out, "The Boss" was plenty solid and it held up for ten years before attachments and other pieces started to break. And, as I mentioned, the actual vacuuming was still great. Thinking back to that memory I reflected that maybe I was being unfair--perhaps the Shark is less study but better in some other way than "The Boss". Then, I put it together (a quick, easy, no-tool process) and realized it was a lot more solid in one piece than it was in a disassembled state. It still wasn't as sturdy as "The Boss" but it was ABOUT HALF THE WEIGHT! AMAZING! Lightweight is good! When I first got "The Boss" I liked the fact that it was heavy and brick-like, I felt it was good for staying glued to the floor as I zipped back and forth over the rugs, floors and carpets. But whenever I had to reposition (maybe to get a tight corner), it meant lifting it up which even for an athletic, then 20-ish, now 30-ish man was a chore. It was HEAVY! So eventually I came to hate the weight. The Shark does not have that problem. I could pick it up and move it into small areas with ease. To summarize, it's plenty sturdy but not as sturdy as my last vacuum. But it is TONS lighter.

I mentioned that lifting the Shark up to get it into tight spaces would be easy; well, the funny thing is, I don't even need to. It really is navigable (guess that's why it's in the Navigator series, duh). But seriously, my old "The Boss" went back-and-forth, then you would have to turn it to hit the carpet at a different angle and go back and forth again. You know...vacuuming! This thing with the Shark is...it feels like you're steering it with the upright stick. This takes a little getting used to since at first while you vacuum, it feels like the vacuum wants to tip over either to the left or right. But it only takes a few minutes to mentally adjust since what's really happening is not that the vacuum wants to tip over, but that my usual movements pushing against the stick to adjust the angle are oversteering the hypersensitive stick. Bottom line, easy to get around. And it sucks so great on the carpet that (as others mentioned) it feels self-propelled. You only have to pull it back, then it will pull itself forward; repeat, repeat, repeat. Cool.

We had arrived at the moment of truth. I have hard floors but an area rug in the living room. I figured I would start there. It had visible pet hair (one dog, one cat) and presumably lots of dirt. I hit it hard like Mike Tyson. Every angle. Every nook and cranny. Even when it was visibly clean I kept going. When it was all said and done, the rug was SPOTLESS!!! Oh, did I forget to mention that I was using my old vacuum? LOL. I wanted to see (in this first experiment) if the Shark vacuum would pick up anything the old one missed. I figured if I could get a little extra dirt, it's worth it. So now I was ready to test the Shark. Time to see if this "no loss of suction" business was for real...

...if anyone had walked into the room after I was done with the Shark, they would have had me committed. I was just sitting there laughing to myself...alone in a room...staring at a vacuum. Maybe the laughter was incredulity, maybe it was happiness (as an uber-consumer I love nothing more than a deal that has value), or maybe I had really snapped. Anyway, staring back at me in the canister--from the rug I had just vacuumed with my prized "The Boss"--was the cause of my temporary mania...three inches of dirt/dust. THREE INCHES!!! I'm talking, like, a crapload of grimy, nasty, gray dirt that I NEVER would have guessed was in that rug. And there it was, staring back at me. As I write this I keep glancing over there just to make sure I'm not nuts...I'm not. THE DIRT IS REAL AND "THE SHARK" IS THE REAL DEAL. IT REALLY SUCKS (in the good way).

So I haven't done the rest of the house but I feel confident enough about the vacuum to write this review now. I CAN'T WAIT to see how much dirt I get when it's all said and done. You know--while researching, I came across a lot of vacuum reviews online by weird vacuum cleaner-loving nerds. It was informative but kinda' creepy watching these dudes walk around in their socks talking about suction. The point I'm getting to is this...the "Shark Rotator Professional Lift-Away" might just turn me (and you) into one of those weirdoes. Is it possible to have feelings for an appliance?

So thanks again to Michael Flux. And thanks to the goofy Shark inventor-dude with the glasses--you are forgiven for the stupid infomercials.

Two final things to mention:

1) My BBnB item said NV501 on the box even though on the Web site it says NV502. At first I was pissed! I had ordered the NV502 after reading the model breakdown by Michael Flux because it was the best bang-for-the-buck and now someone had sent me an NV501. Then I noticed that there was a sticker that said NV502 on it. NV501? NV502? I don't know. I guess the main thing is to make sure it has all the parts you are expecting (please refer to the Michael Flux review) and if it does, it's an NV502. As near as I can figure, the NV502 is just an NV501 with the car detailing kit thrown inside the box and an "NV502" sticker placed on the outside of the box.

2) On the topic of included attachments, my vacuum cleaner came with MORE than expected. I'm not sure if this is because I got an NV501 rebadged as an NV502 or if the attachments are different regionally or if Michael Flux was missing a piece or if I got something extra. But anyway I got the following:

EXPECTED ATTACHMENTS - PART NUMBER
Detailing Kit - XCD350W
18-inch Flexi Crevice Tool - X12FC500
Multi-Tool (brush/furniture) - X10FC500
Straight Suction Floor Nozzle - XSN500
Premium (large) air powered turbo brush - X13FC500

UNEXPECTED ATTACHMENT:
Multi-Angle dusting brush - X15FC500

I was under the impression that the multi-angle dusting tool would not be included but I got it somehow. With regard to that part, I'M GOING TO DISAGREE WITH MICHAEL FLUX ON ONE POINT HERE. He said that the multi-angle dusting brush (in the NV501) was not as favorable as the detailing kit (in the NV502) because the multi-tool can also do the dusting. While I agree with that statement, I would go with the NV501 in the following scenarios 1) you don't own a car (obviously) OR 2) you plan to do most of your dusting with the vacuum OR 3) you have a lot of delicate surfaces that you dust. Let me better explain reasons two and three...

Had I not gotten the multi-angle dusting brush by mistake, I would have been fine with just the multi-tool. The multi-tool seems more-than-capable of dusting most surfaces. But since I got the duster by mistake (?), I can see where it has value. It is at least twice as wide as the multi-tool so you can dust surfaces more quickly; as mentioned, if you use a vacuum for most of your dusting it makes sense. Also, the bristles on the duster are softer than the bristles on the multi-tool so if I have a lot of of knickknacks, bric-a-brac or frames I would prefer the duster as opposed to the detailer. The multi-tool is great on shelves, furniture, etc however the bristles are about the firmness of a soft-bristled toothbrush so I wouldn't (for example) use it on a flat screen TV surface but I would use the multi-angle duster whose bristles are even softer. This might be a bad example since most people (including myself) use a damp cloth to clean their TV surface because they want to remove smudges and fingerprints in addition to dust. But you get the idea...the multi-tool is not abrasive but it's not as soft as the duster.

I still agree with Michael Flux that the NV502 is the best bargain. My personal suggestion if you haven't already bought the vacuum would be to follow his advice and get the NV502 from BBnB, then cross your fingers and hope to get the multi-angle duster included like I did. If you don't, the multi-tool is great and you'll probably never know what you're missing but should you feel like you need a duster that's gentler or wider than the multi-tool, just order part# X15FC500 (multi-angle dusting brush) online somewhere or even the "Universal Dusting Brush" that pivots but doesnt angle. Conversely, if you buy the NV501 (here on Amazon for example) but want to detail your car with your awesome new vacuum, just order part # XCD350W (detailing kit) somewhere online.

So there's my review...the reason I gave this 4-stars instead of 5-stars is because of the durability issue. It's very good but not excellent...it could be a tad bit better. Also, the "fit and finish" (an automobile term which simply means the way the different parts align and fit together as a working unit) is not 100%...it's probably 95%. Though 95% is great for whatever overseas sweatshop it was made in. So 5-stars on sucking power, 4-stars on construction, 6-stars on value. GREAT VACUUM!!!

PS. AFTER you get your BBnB code but BEFORE you go to their Web site to purchase, go to your ebates account first to get redirected to BBnB. This will earn you cash back on your purchase (currently only 1% but it's better than nothing). If you don't have an ebates account, sign up for free, they give up to 30% off at online merchants including Amazon; and when they ask who referred you, please use my email: ABANKSNYC AT YAHOO DOT COM.

SO FROM NOW ON, WHEN YOU PLAN TO BUY SOMETHING ONLINE
1) go to retailmenot for a promo code but don't click any links (it will give them a commission instead of you and your ebates account)
2) go to your ebates account and choose the store you plan to shop at, you will be redirected to your shopping site
3) Make your purchase and use your promo code.
4) Save money

I use this method to MURDER KOHL'S when I shop. I always wait for a 30% off coupon at retailmenot (they usually pop up every two months). Then I go to ebates and click through to Kohls (right now they are giving 6% cash back). So it's like saving 36%. For example, if I was going to buy the NV501 vacuum at Kohls that I just reviewed, it is listed at $299 but I would wait for the 30% off promo code and throw in my 6% cash back and SAVE OVER $100 ($299 minus 30% = $209.30 minus 6% cash back through ebates = net cost of $196.74). Ebates rocks. Oh, and Kohls lets you use up to two promo codes at checkout so in addition to the 30% off, I forgot to mention, there is always a free shipping promo always floating around.

But anyway, BUY THIS VACUUM.
