top_rant.text:
So much has already been written, I will simply hit my experience in one month of ownership.

It was between this and VitaMix (VM) at $400-$500 (I rapidly ruled out Ninjas based on even their highest level machine having plastic coupling of blade to motor (underneath the cup) and the reviews saying they pretty much crap out suddenly after a few months). I'm delighted I got this for Smoothies. If I was a professional chef, making pastry dough, perhaps I would need the VM. I make only Smoothies.

This easily smoothies ice cubes, frozen tangerines and bananas, etc. I freeze everything now--including spinach-in-a-bag, leftover yogurt, etc.--and just grind it in this Oster to a smooth pulp. I see no residual skin, pulp, etc.

This machine even has a dedicated button for smoothies--it works for a minute or so, then turns off by itself. You can do other things...it just runs perfectly. Nice!

This machine has no tamper stick like VM, the kind that one sticks in the top to mush food around. Why? It doesn't need one--the blade works in both directions, even when you hit the Smoothie button (ten seconds one way, ten seconds the other, etc. automatically). So, food doesn't get stuck, and it does a swell job. Might it be tougher on the motor to be bi-directional? See warranty note later in this review.

Adding water, and how much, is the key to smoothie making. Too much, too thin; not enough, hard time making smoothie. You'll get the hang of it.

I do not use the To Go cups bc they don't hold enough solids to make enough drink for my tastes. I haven't tried the food processor and likely never will.

This machine, just like VM brand, has a 7-year warranty! Basically, you pay to return the machine, they replace it and ship you a new or refurbed one (at least that's what it says for both VM and Oster online; I haven't tried it).

Key: the Oster official website sells replacement parts. The container, the blade, the gaskets ($1 each) and the lid are, all together, about $50. They have free shipping over $50. I love that I can replace the blade, or the gasket, should it be needed. I bought a whole "replacement kit" of these items from Oster. Comparatively, the VitaMix item--the full set that I described--are $125!! [[ASIN:B001949US4 Vitamix Eastman Tritan Copolyester Soft-Grip 64-Ounce Container with Wet Blade and Lid]] (VM parts can be seen on Amazon). Bust a VM blade and you'll be springing for the whole enchilada since the blade is built into the blending container base; Oster allows single parts to be replaced (I think the new blade was $6.99).

Clean up is a snap; run lid under water to wash it, then half fill the Oster container with water, blend on high for 15 seconds, open to air dry. Boom! No taking off the blades, or the rubber ring, or all that jazz. Cleanup takes under a minute, so I do it before I even drink my smoothie.

It is powerful at 1100-watts (normal $40 blender is about 250-watts). I debated the Oster $199 model with the 1400-watt motor, but for smoothies, this one is ideal. Strong! Ice---no match! If one is a chef, maybe consider the 1400-watter to knead dough or make thick peanut butter or something. For the smoothie guys, this is enough.

It's loud. I got a pair of earplugs to keep by the blender, no joke! Love these earplugs, and I suggest you order them with this blender unless you just want to put your two fingers in your ears each time, which is cool, too. [[ASIN:B001DZO87M Howard Leight by Honeywell R-01538 Quiet Band Hearing Protector with Reusable Pods]] These earplugs fit perfectly around the base for storage--no kidding!

Tip: I considered a "juicer", but those are a beeeatch to clean, and I want pulp and rind. Sure, it's a touch thicker to drink, but I have had no issues. None at all. There is so much waste with juicers. Here, no waste. Grind seeds and all. The thickness depends on how much water one adds (fruit to water ratio).

I tried Almond Milk as a base, but it rots in one week in the fridge, and cold water is so easy, now I just use tap water. Soy milk, cow's milk, you can use any of it that you have lying around.

I add protein power, like Vanilla or Chocolate. Sure, I could add pea protein or nut protein, and would if I had them lying around, but in a quick minute, just toss in a scoop of the stuff bodybuilders use. And sweetener, the videos online would tell you bee honey or what not, and I tried it, but in the end, good old sugar or Sweet and Lo and this thing's sweet enough.

You can get so many pre-diced frozen fruits in the store. Strawbs, pomegranate, cherrys, blah blah. Sure, I use fresh when I can, but to get a bag of, say, pineapple chunks (frozen and pre-cut) and toss them in here with a rotten banana and yesterday's unfinished dinner salad and, bam, some chocolate protein power to kill off the skunky taste--man, it's like Thanksgiving in Key West every day!

I like that you don't have to take the stems off the strawberries or nothing. Seeds in your oranges--who gives a rat's patoot. And you know those oranges that you peel and it's like, darn, I'm leaving more white rind on here than I'm taking off. "Shed your skin, you crazy little, out-of-season Sunkist!" Well, before it was painful to eat that rubbery thing, knowing that you spent, like, a buck on the big ones and darn if you're going to toss it away--now, bam, just chuck it in the Oster "garbage disposal for the gullet" and it minces up so you can gulp it down like drinking a fruity picante. You win!

Whenever we buy fruit, Jello cups, pudding, whatever, it all gets brown and old before we can eat it. For instance, yogurt: you know that "Oh, poo! Today's the expiration date, and I have a ten-mile hike this afternoon. Should I chance it?" Now, no problem...pop that bad boy in the freezer. In a week or so, when you're just lounging around watching the NY Jets eat sod yet again, spoon that vintage yogurt into the blender with a crummy packet of Swiss Miss Cocoa mix that's been growing cobwebs in your pantry, add some water, and beat the living daylight out of the thing. Then throw in some cashews that have been greasing up the bottom of a dish on the footstool for a couple weeks, plus a few of those licorice jellybeans that no one wants to ever eat, and bam, hit the smoothie button and go take a leak. It runs itself and shuts itself off. Come back in a couple minutes and, voila, dinner is served!

This blender comes with a fancy "cookbook", but unless your kitchen has a ton of plantains, Valencia grapes, papayas and kiwi lying around, you'll likely just end up throwing some bananas in this baby and calling it a day.

It's liberating to know that those "Hey, those bananas are looking pretty rancid!" moments can be tamed; just peel them, throw them in a gallon freezer bag, and boom, smoothie them anytime. Enjoy salads? I now buy bags of 1/2 priced salad--you know, the type with the big flourescent stickers that say "Buy Me!", but that ought to say, "I'm poison" because in, like, twelve hours it'll become compost right there in your grocer's freezer--well, buy a few bags just to freeze! Between getting my salads half price, plus using no salad dressing, I figure I'll have this blender bought and paid for in, like, a year and a half.

Bloobs for cereal, can't eat them before getting fuzzy, right? Now just freeze them and, in month, make a blueberry-vanilla protein powder smoothie. Toss in some Gushers Fruit Snacks that your kids left lying around and it's delish! Booyah!

Be the master of your kitchen again, the way it was supposed to be!!

If I drink a smoothie, plus eat a frozen macs and cheese Smart One and a 1/2 jumbo bag of Cool Ranch Doritos (to add some crunch *wink, wink*), I'm good for hours and hours. Maybe even all night (but, admittedly, at midnight I'm too tired to make another smoothie, so I just eat a Ring-Ding and call it done).

I drank 1-2 smoothies a day for a month and it has changed my life. They are nutritious and filling. This review is going to make you think I've lost my marbles, but I swear on a stack of Sports Illustrated magazines that I have ground up and drank everything that I have mentioned in this review. This thing is intense! You'll start putting your hand behind the couch cushions just looking for things to grind up and drink.

I suspected that I might get the ka-poops with all that pulpy fiber coursing through my colon like a tractor-trailer truck on a lonely highway, but it hasn't happened. Maybe, like my ex-wife said, it was the Devil Dogs and Chicago Red Hot frankfurters that were tearing me up. All-natural doesn't seem to twink me out as much as I guessed it would.

Yes, I tossed in a jigger of honey Jack once or twice. My brother, Mark, turned me on to the stuff and I'm a lightweight when it comes to booze. But, with icing, this baby is cold anyhow, why not? So sue me. I'm human. Honey Jack D likes the Oster, too. Never complained, anyway. Hope this review helped someone who is on the fence about living a healthy lifestyle. :-)

Recommended!

UPDATE: have owned it now for nearly four months, no issues. Used daily. Smoothies. Awesome! But when sticking hand in couch cushions to find things to grind and drink, here's something I learned the hard way: it's not powerful enough to grind Washington quarters and copper pennies.

#. #### ####

Don Brunkhardt

Email #4 At this point I was mad because they have no parts and after spending a total of $140ish less than a year ago they want another $85 total for another unit. I see a pattern here don,t you.

On 10/23/2010 9:49 AM, #### #### wrote:

Your warranty time is very short by most any ones standard. 90 day unconditional guarantee I understand in order to sell sounds great if you don't like it send it back with in 90 days. After freight for replacement you want another $85.00 that's over $200.00 in less than a year. If you can't do better than that then your product and company are not very good and I will be sure to let everyone know. May be I should put a how to build your own shower manager for free on the internet also. A step by step how to make your own. All anyone needs is a ¾ sprinkler valve $12.00, 24VAC plug in transformer, 555 dual timer rectifier, some resistors, caps, Regulator, thermal switch ($20.00). free step by step instructions. Less than $50.00 total to accomplish the same thing by shutting off the hot water or by using a Normally open valve to screw onto the shower head like you do. I have had cheap sprinkler valves last over 10 years now. If your product won't even last a year then it is JUNK. Think about it from my prospective and not your cut off the nose to spite the face for sake of making more profit prospective. Karma is alive and well I have thought about it and the answer is no way will I spend another $85.00 for a replacement. If you can't do better than that, I will make my own and share the instructions with the world all the while dogging you with not so pleasant reviews and links to a copy of this email transactions plastered on a URL.

Email #5 they respond with this statement that really pissed me off.

I don't know what road you are talking about, but we should remind you that our product / concept is protected by two U.S Patents and infringement of these rights are aggressively pursued through legal recourse.

Shower Manager, LLC

Let me be the first to inform you that you cannot place a patent concept infringement suit against any person who is not profiting. What I get out of this the peace of mind knowing that maybe I save some people from the same grief.

My advice for you is to either make a better product that will last a reasonable amount of time, or offer a warrant sufficient to allow a person to get their monies worth out of the product. Since you are unwilling to reach any form of reasonable negotiation, I will try to get some reviews out there first chance I get. It will take me a little time because I am busy with some projects, but be on the Lookout for the FREE step by step how to build your own shower timer with photos coming soon to a internet near you. I will be sure to give you honorable mention for inspiring me to produce them.

Sincerely,

Pissed off

I figured out a way better deterrent for forcing my kids to not waste the hot water. I installed a Nice ¼ turn brass ball valve on the hot water tank outlet. I tell them that they have 10 minutes to get a shower. You won't have turn off the hot valve over twice because we have nice 60 deg. well water. It seems to be the cheapest easiest thing possible to do and it only cost about $10.00 and any one can do it. Just go to the Lows/Depot and tell them the type pipe and size you have. They have a ¼ turn valve with something called a Gator bite connection on each end. You won't even need glue just cut a place to insert the valve. Seriously the kids will get the point in a way they hate.

top_screaming.text:
GOT THE UNIT ABOUT A WEEK AGO, HAVE BEEN USING IT EVERY DAY SINCE. OVERALL THE UNIT HAS GREAT FEATURES AND GIVES YOU A FAIRLY GOOD DISPLAY OF THE 4 MODES AND READINGS OF VOLTAGE, mA, mAh AND ELAPSED TIME FOR EACH CELL. IT WOULD HAVE BEEN NICE IF THE DISPLAY WAS A LITTLE LARGER IT'S A LITTLE HARD TO SEE (I.M NOT YOUNG!).....

ONE GLITCH I'M COMING ACROSS IS SETTING THE "DISCHARGE CURRENT" . I'LL CALL THE LEFT MOST BATTERY SLOT 1 AND THE RIGHT MOST SLOT 4. WHEN INSTALL THE FIRST BATTERY IN SLOT 1 SET THE MODE TO DISCHARGE AND SET THE DISCHARGE CURRENT TO 350 mA. I WAIT UNTIL THE BLINKING STOPS AND INSTALL THE SECOND BATTERY IN SLOT 2, SET TO "DISCHARGE" AND SET THE "CURRENT TO "350mA". IT'S WORKING GREAT, BUT WHEN I INSTALL THE 3RD BATTERY IN SLOT 3 (OR SOMETIMES 4TH SLOT) AND WHEN I SET IT TO "DISCHARGE" MODE AND TRY TO SET THE CURRENT, THE SLOT 2 DISPLAY WILL BLINK SIMULTANEOUSLY WITH SLOT 3 (SOMETIMES IT WILL DO WHEN I INSERT THE 4TH BATTERY IN SLOT 4) AND THE MODE IS AUTOMATICALLY CHANGED TO "CHARGE" MODE... SO I HAVE TO KEEP TAKING OUT THE SLOT 2 BATTERY (3 OR 4) AND KEEP CONTINUING FROM BEGINNING UNTIL THE BC-700 TAKES ALL 4 BATTERY AT THE DISCHARGING RATE AT 350 mA.

I HAVEN'T TESTED THE OTHER MODES, BUT I THINK THEY ARE OK. IT'S JUST THE STRANGE MODE CHANGE ON IT'S OWN THAT IS IRRITATING. ALSO WHEN I PLUG IN THE BC-700 TO THE POWER SUPPLY THE DISPLAY READS FROM SLOT 1 "92" SLOT 2 "89" SLOT 3 "700" SLOT 4 "36" I WONDER IF IT'S VERSION 36?

ANYWAY IT'S DOING THE JOB OF RECONDITIONING THE BATTERIES AND THAT'S THE MAIN THING I WANTED. ALTHOUGH THE "NEED" TO KEEP REPROGRAMMING CORRECTLY TO GET THE CORRECT MODE AND CURRENT SETTING IS A PAIN, SOMETIMES IT REVERTS TO THE LOWEST 100 mA AND WHEN I'M NOT AWARE IT TAKES SO LONG TO GO THROUGH THE CYCLE. I WISH THIS UNIT DIDN'T HAVE A MIND OF IT'S OWN!

UPDATE FEBRUARY 9 2013: I THINK I GOT THE ABOVE PROBLEM CLEARED! OK, IT SAYS DURING THE "8 SECONDS" WE SET THE "MODE" AND "DISCHARGE/CHARGE RATE". RIGHT AFTER THE BLINKING STOPS AFTER THE 8 SECONDS, WATCH THE DISPLAY REAL GOOD. AFTER ABOUT 2 TO 3 SECONDS AFTER THE 8 SECOND FLASHING STOPS THE DISPLAY WILL FLASH ONLY ONCE AND THAT IS WHEN THE ACTUAL CHARGE/DISCHARGE BEGINS! (YOU CAN TELL BY LOOKING AT THE DISPLAY THAT STARTS TO GIVE READING LIKE 702 mA, NOT A SOLID 200/500/700 mA) NOT AFTER THE 8 SECONDS. IF YOU INSERT ANOTHER BATTERY IN BETWEEN THE 8 SECOND AND THE 2-3 SECOND SINGLE BLINK YOU WILL HAVE THE PROBLEM I HAD ABOVE.

SO AFTER INSERTING THE BATTERY AND PRESS THE 1-4 SWITCH, THE DISPLAY WILL START TO FLASH, SET THE MODE AND CURRENT TO DESIRED mA). PLEASE DO NOT INSERT ANOTHER BATTERY IN THE SECOND OR THE NEXT SLOT UNTIL YOU SEE THE FLASHING STOPS AND YOU SEE JUST ONE BLINK, THEN YOU CAN INSERT MORE CELLS.

I HOPE PEOPLE WILL UNDERSTAND WHAT I'M TRYING TO SAY. WITH THIS FINDING AND NO OTHER PROBLEMS, I WOULD GIVE IT A FOUR AND A HALF STAR.

IF THE BASIC UNIT WAS A LITTLE LARGER IT WILL BE EASIER INSERTING/REMOVING THE BATTERY FROM THE BC-700. ALSO IF THE STATUS DISPLAY WAS LARGER IT WOULD BE GREAT!

IT'S A GREAT LITTLE BATTERY RECONDITIONING DEVICE TO HAVE IN THESE DAYS OF RECHARGEABLE BATTERIES! I WOULD LIKE TO TRY THE BC-1000 WITH HIGHER AMPERE HOUR CAPACITY IN THE FUTURE. THANKS FOR THE GREAT PRODUCT!

***UPDATE APRIL 16, 2013

HAVE BEEN USING THE CHARGER AND HAVE NOTICED THAT THE THREE PUSH BUTTON SWITCH AND ONE OF THE BUTTON IS GIVING ME PROBLEM... "CURRENT", "DISPLAY" AND "MODE". YES IT'S THE "CURRENT" PUSH BUTTON (I REMEMBER SOMEONE WITH THE SAME PROBLEM) AND I HAVE RUN INTO THE PROBLEM OF GETTING A VERY VERY HARD TIME TO CHANGE THE CURRENT SETTING (I DON'T KNOW HOW LONG THE SWITCH IS GOING TO LAST, HOPEFULLY LONG). WELL THE FEEL OF THE THREE SWITCH DEFINITELY FEELS CHEAP, I HOPE IT WON'T GET STUCK ON ONE CURRENT SETTING. OTHERWISE THE CHARGER IS DOING A GREAT JOB OF CHARGING/DISCHARGING/CONDITIONING THE BATTERIES. I WISH THEY USED A BETTER SWITCH SINCE IT GETS USED A LOT! CUTTING CORNER ON THE THREE SWITCH'S WAS A MISTAKE.

THIS IS A GREAT MACHINE. BACKGROUND, I HAD A GAGGIA SYNCHRONY SUPERAUTOMATIC FOR 5 YEARS, THEN SOLD IT. GOOD ESPRESSO, I THOUGHT AT THE TIME. BEEN A STARBUCKS DRINKER FOR TEN YEARS IN CHICAGO BEFORE GETTING MY FIRST ESPRESSO MAKER. CHARBUCKS IS MEDIOCRE, BUT THEY SHOWED ME THE POSSIBILITIES. I WAS LUCKY THAT MY GAGGIA TITANIUM LASTED 6 YEARS AND 10,000 SHOTS.
THE TITANIUM SUPER AUTO MADE A SLIGHTLY BETTER SHOT THAN THE SYNCHRONY. I SPENT ABOUT 100 HOURS DOING RESEARCH ON SEMI-AUTO MACHINES, BECAUSE I REALLY BELIEVE ITS THE WAY TO GREAT ESPRESSO.
ROCKET CROSSED MY MIND, BUT THE MESS AND BOTHER SEEMED TOO MUCH .
I SAW TONS OF VIDEOS ON THE BREVILLE ORACLE MAKING ME BELIEVER. THIS IS THE ONLY HYBRID MACHINE THAT WORKS THIS SMOOTHLY , MAKING EXCELLENT COFFEE, WITH LITTLE FUSS. . BEEN TO TOP GRADE ROASTERS AND PLACED MYSELF IN FRONT OF BARRISTA SUPERSTARS ON $10,000 MACHINES. SOME SHOTS WERE GOOD OR GREAT, AND SOME BITTER AND SOUR. $2000 HURT A LOT, BUT I AM SUPER HAPPY. THE RELIABILITY RATING BY AMAZON AND OTHER TEST SITES WAS NEAR 80%. ESPRESSO MACHINES SEEM TO HAVE A 50-60% CUSTOMER PROBLEM RATIO. I PULLED MY FIRST FEW SHOTS WITH A POUND OF SPECIALTY JAVA ESPRESSO AUTHENTICO. IT WAS ROASTED 5 DAYS AGO. I SET MY THE BOILER TO 199 DEGREE TO STAY AWAY FROM A BITTER SHOT. TAMP PRESSURE 3 TAMP DURATION 4 I TRIED 20 SEC SINGLE SHOT, JUST TO TASTE IT. GREAT CREMA AND THE LEAST BITTERNESS OF ANY SHOT I EVER HAD. THEN TRIED A DOUBLE SHOT 28 SEC AND 30ML.
THE POUR OUT OF THE SPOUTS ARRIVED AT THE 11 SECOND MARK. THE DOUBLE WAS VERY SMOOTH . I LOVE THIS MACHINE , SO GLAD I GOT IT. DID NOT SEEM TO HAVE A LOT OF TROUBLE GETTING GOOD SHOTS. MAYBE A LITTLE LUCK OR
SOME EXPERIENCE. I LIKE THAT IT IS NOT MESSY AND EASY TO PROGRAM AND MAKE INSTANT CHANGES. I LOVE THE LOOK OF THE ROCKET 58 , BUT THIS IS THE MACHINE. FRESH BEANS ROASTED TO MED DARK AND 6-20 DAY OLD ROAST DATE.
CLEAN WATER WITH LESS THAN 100 PPM MINERALS. ALSO JUST ORDERED A BOTTOMLESS PORTAFILTER TO SEE SHOT
PRODUCTION AND TWEAK SHOTS MORE. IF THERE BE A NEGATIVE, IS THAT THE DOSAGE CAN'T BE CHANGED.

BECAUSE YOU SEE NONE .... LEAVING THAT MYSTERY ALONE FOR AWHILE YOU NOW SCOPE OUT THE CAN SENSOR BAR ,A HALF INCH OF A TIN RODLIKE SWITCH WHICH,WHEN IT FEELS THE CAN RIM PRESS IN ON IT, THE THIRD STEP, PUSHING THE 'START' BUTTON IS POSSIBLE ... EXCEPT THERE IS NO WAY ... NEVER .... WILL ANY CAN RIM MAKE IT SO CLOSE TO THAT SENSOR BAR TO TRIP IT . PUSH THE CAN AS HARD AS YOU WANT, THAT SWITCH REMAINS HOVERING NEARLY AN EIGHTH OF AN INCH OUT OF REACH ....

ALWAYS THE OPTIMIST YOU TURN TO THE BUYER REVIEWS, CHUCKLING TO YOURSELF ABOUT THE HAPPY THOUGHT OF READING PEOPLE'S COMPLAINTS AND FRUSTRATION AT THIS IMPOSSIBLE PIECE OF GARBAGE DEVICE SO MANY HAVE INVESTED IN ... LET'S SOLVE THIS CASE WATSON !

TURNING THE BOTTOM MECHANISMS OF DUAL WHEELS, LEVERS, RIVETS AND PIVOTS REVEALS NOTHING SPECIAL .... BUT WAIT ! WHY IS THE CUTTING WHEEL NEARLY 25% OVER THE TOP EDGE OF THE SPIKED DRIVER WHEEL ? THATS MORE THE USUAL POSITION WHEN ANY ELECTRIC CAN OPENER IS ...WELL ... OPENING !

SAAAY ! THIS CAN OPENER WAS PACKED AND SHIPPED WITH THE MECHANICS ALREADY IN THE POSITIONS THEY TAKE NOT WHEN AT REST, BUT WHEN ACTUALLY PERFORMING !!! YOU PRESS THE RIM SENSOR SWITCH IN WITH YOUR FINGERTIP. NOTHING HAPPENS SO YOU ADD ANOTHER TASK AND ALSO PRESS THE START BUTTON WHILE STILL DEPRESSING THE SENSOR TIN BAR ... THE BEAST STARTS ! AND BECAUSE YOU ALSO READ IT IS SUPPOSED TO RUN AND STOP ITSELF WHEN THE JOB IS OVER, YOU BECOME DETERMINED TO KEEP BOTH SWITCHES IN THE ON POSITION ..... 60 SECONDS LATER AS IT NEARS ITS TURN OFF TIME, ...MIRACLE OF MIRACLES .... THE CUTTING WHEEL MOVES BACK AND AWAY FROM OVERLAPPING THE SPIKED DRIVE GEAR ! THERE IS THE BLASTED GROOVE ! AND THE CAN WILL MOST CERTAINLY TOUCH AND TRIP THE NOW REACHABLE SENSOR !!! OMG ! i'M EXHAUSTED FROM RELAYING THIS MINI ADVENTURE ... BUT IF YOU BUY ONE REMEMBER ! THE CUTTING WHEEL SHOULD NOT BE 25% OVER TOP OF THE DRIVING GEAR LIKE SOME JUST STARTING SOLAR ECLIPSE .... POWER IT ON AND KEEP IT ON TILL IT MOVES ITS BLADE BACK AND AWAY FROM CONCIELING THE TOP OF THE DRIVE GEAR AND YOUR CAN OPENING LIFE WILL NO LONGER MAKE YOU FLIP YOUR LID !!!

top_screaming.text:
I AM GOING TO LEAVE MY INITIAL REVIEW, JUST BELOW, BUT WANT TO EXPLAIN MY IRE.

ONE OF MY NEIGHBORS JUST PUT ONE OF THESE FANS, ALSO IN BLACK, OUT AS A ''FREE FOR THE TAKING.'' SINCE I WAS PLANNING TO GET ANOTHER FAN, MOST PROBABLY ONE OF THESE, FOR THE GUEST BEDROOM, I TOOK IT HOME. AND THEN I DISCOVERED HOW THE MFGER HAS CHEAPENED DOWN THE DESIGN.

IT TURNS OUT THAT THE SHAFT BETWEEN THE MECHANISM AND THE BASE USED TO BE ROUND, AS IT IS ON THE FREEBIE I JUST ACQUIRED.... AND AT THE BOTTOM OF IT, THERE IS A "WRISTBAND" THAT IS ABOUT 1'' TALL AND GOES ALL AROUND THAT SHAFT. IT HAS SETTINGS FROM ZERO (WHICH IS THE OFF POSITION) TO FOUR, THUS ALLOWING FOR ALL THE SPEED OPTIONS THAT THE CURRENT MODEL PROVIDES. NO NEED TO REACH BEHIND TO ACCESS IT. IT IS DIRECTLY IN FRONT.

ON THAT OLDER MODEL, THE ONLY NEED TO REACH BEHIND THE BLADES IS TO CHOOSE BETWEEN OSCILLATING AND NOT OSCILLATING. HAVING TO KEEP REACHING BEHIND TO TURN MY FAN ON AND OFF HAS REALLY ANNOYED ME. I HAVE OWNED IT FOR OVER A MONTH AND A HALF AND MY IRRITATION HAS NOT DIMINISHED ONE BIT. HENCE MY REDUCTION TO THREE STARS, WHICH I REALLY OUGHT TO HAVE DONE ANYWAY AT THIS POINT. I CONSOLE MYSELF WITH THE THOUGHT THAT THE UNIVERSE HAS GIVEN ME A CONSOLATION PRIZE.

I WILL USE THIS NEW ONE IN MY BEDROOM AND GRACIOUSLY MAKE THE SWITCH TO THE GUEST BEDROOM WHEN IT IS OCCUPIED BY GUESTS.
___________________________________________________

HERE IS MY ORIGINAL REVIEW, ALL OF WHICH IS STILL HOW I FEEL ABOUT THIS FAN. JUST KNOW THAT YOU ARE BEING SHORT-CHANGED IF YOU BUY IT. BUT IT'S STILL PROBABLY THE BEST OF WHAT AMAZON HAS TO OFFER AND THE BLACK..WHICH COSTS MORE FOR SOME REASON.. IS QUITE STYLISH, I THINK.

THERE ARE NIGHTS WHEN I USE A FAN EVEN THOUGH IT ISN'T HOT. I LIKE THE AIR MOVEMENT AND THE WHITE NOISE. I HAVE BEEN USING MY OLD LASKO WINDOW FAN FOR AT LEAST 12 YEARS AND IT IS STILL FUNCTIONING PERFECTLY. BUT, I NEEDED SOMETHING SMALLER FOR NIGHTS WHEN THE WINDOW IS CLOSED; IT JUST TAKES UP TOO MUCH ROOM. SO, I READ REVIEWS CAREFULLY AND DECIDED TO STICK WITH LASKO. I BOUGHT THE BLACK ONE.
I AM VERY PLEASED WITH THIS FAN'S INITIAL OPERATION. AS OTHERS HAVE MENTIONED, IT MOVES A GOODLY AMOUNT OF AIR, EVEN ON THE NUMBER ONE SETTING...THE SAME SETTING I TYPICALLY USED FOR THE WINDOW FAN EXCEPT UNDER HOT CONDITIONS. THE WHITE NOISE PRODUCED ON THAT LEVEL IS ALSO IDEAL FOR ME, THOUGH SIGNIFICANTLY QUIETER THAN THE LARGER FAN
I SEE NO NEED FOR THE OSCILLATING FUNCTION SO I PULLED THE LITTLE STACK-LIKE THING ON THE BACK OF THE FAN AND HAVE IT ONLY IN STATIONARY POSITION.
IF THE DIAGRAMS ON THE INSTRUCTIONS WERE A BIT MORE OBVIOUS, IT WOULD HAVE HELPED. THAT AND IF I HADN'T MADE IT MORE COMPLICATED THAN IT NEEDED TO BE. OH WELL, RIGHT?
BOTTOM LINE..THIS IS A WORTHWHILE PURCHASE AND I DON'T THINK YOU'LL BE DISAPPOINTED.
ALSO..IT'S AESTHETICALLY PLEASING IN ITS FASHIONABLY BLACK FINISH. "EVERY ROOM NEEDS A LITTLE BASIC BLACK," OR SO I'VE BEEN TOLD. SEEMS TRUE. AND MUCH NICER TO LOOK AT THAN THE WINDOW FAN.

I have a septic system and can't install a garbage disposal, so I keep a screen trap in my sink to catch coffee grounds, fruit peels, and the like. Like another reviewer, I had a white plastic coated wire trash bag holder attached to the door under my sink. It was flimsy and hard to clean, but it was so useful to have a trash bin that I could access with one finger. When it finally fell apart, boy did I miss it.... and had dirty spots trailing across the floor from trying to carry wet gunk from the sink to the trash can 6 feet away. I found a crappy one that was even more flimsy and totally unacceptable--the lid would not stay open when lifted, which made it useless for transferring garbage when your hands are full. I looked EVERYWHERE for a replacement like that wire one, but none of the local kitchen stores seem to carry them anymore. Nor could I find any online... until I found this handy and far superior item from SimpleHuman.

This is just great. I love that it comes with a storage bin for the bags, and that the bin catches any drippings from the bags. Many of those shopping bags have little holes in the seams on the bottom, so I always double bag... but sometimes they still leak. No problem, because the bin that holds bags underneath has a tray-style top to catch any drippings. Very smart thinking!

I think that the people who complained about the measurements just looked at height and width of the unit vs. size of their cabinet door. Perhaps they failed to consider the DEPTH of the bin (8+ inches) when they measured their cabinet doors... you have to account for the lid in order to ensure that your cabinet door is wide enough and tall enough to open and close with the bin in place.

Be sure to take a piece of cardboard that equals the size of the bin cover-- 13.2x 8.2 inches--and hold it perpendicular to the cabinet door where you expect the lid to be--and make sure that you can open and close the door. Don't just measure your cabinet door, because the actual OPENING that the door covers will be smaller. I don't think that simplehuman can be blamed for not including that advice in the specs, but they might be wise to do so to minimize returns. I was very careful to test before I drilled holes for the screws. I would recommend having a friend help hold it in place as you do so, as it's hard to hold steadily in place with one hand while you mark where the holes go. But I managed to get the holes marked correctly and now I have a very solid, very useful under-sink bin.

These days I use cloth bags when I shop so I try not to accumulate those plastic shopping bags, but somehow I always have them around. If I run out, I'll grab a few from the self-checkout or the bag-recycle bin at the store.

BTW they also have excellent customer service. I managed to break one of the three brackets that hold the lid to the steel frame. I don't mean to imply that the lid is brittle or weak because it's not. The plastic is solid but flexible. Perhaps I was a bit rough in pulling it off when I became impatient about trying to measure those drill holes by myself. Perhaps it came that way, I don't know. I could have let ti go, as the other two brackets held it in place and it worked fine, but I wrote to simplehuman... and they immediately sent me a replacement lid.

I could not be happier, which is why I am bothering to write. I'm glad I bought an extra one as a gift for a friend. Excellent product.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Love the SimpleHuman trash system!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
We have been needing a new kitchen trash can and I selected this one as a replacement for the one we have been using. I have not worked with a motion-activated trash can before this one so I wasn’t sure if there was anything more complex than just waving a hand in front of it and having it pop open. No, that’s as complex as it gets. So far we’re pleased with it.

This is a stainless steel can with a battery-powered lid. The motion sensor is on the top and is obvious (plus, it is marked with a sticker right out of the box). When the batteries are inserted properly (6 AA-batteries), a simple wave of the hand activates the can and the lid pops open. I found that a paper plate is also sufficient, so I don’t think it’s infrared. My concern, then, was that it would open every time someone walked by it, but this has not been the case because of the location and angling of the sensor.

Operation is very smooth and quiet. Unless you’re in the room with the trash can and are listening for it, the lid opening and closing is very nearly silent. When it is triggered, the lid flips up and stays open for about 5 seconds before it closes again. The opening into the trash bag below is wide enough that you likely will never need to touch the can or its contents until you take out the bag. A 13-gallon kitchen bag is sufficient and seems to be ideal.

Our old can had a foot-operated lid. It also kept us from touching the can with our hands, but it often made the can move and turn when we opened it and I found myself turning it back around so I could open it which defeats the purpose. This is not an issue with this new can.

The top does not lock down, it simply rests on top of the can so it is easy to remove and replace. I can see a potential issue with this. For me, this is not a problem and is actually convenient, but for those that have dogs that are smart and that love to dig in the trash, the non-locking lid will be no challenge to keep out a determined canine. While I do not consider this a flaw, a possible minor design improvement might be a latch system to firmly attach the lid.

So far we’re very pleased with this trash can. The motion sensor works well and it operates quietly. The good capacity is a plus. Based on my experience with this trash can, I have no problems recommending it.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Quiet Operation and Good Capacity

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Excellent quality, great design, pleasing appearance. The best residential trash can I have seen or owned.

The only quibble is they can't use normal 13 gallon trash bags from the supermarket without modification. The pedal mechanism makes a big hump in the bottom of the can, with two deep holes on either side. A normal trash bag is pulled off the rim when trash is put into the bag and forces it to fall down into these holes. Filling the holes with foam or sand or kitty litter would allow normal bags to be used, but it would be messy. I found that twisting a normal trash bags edge until it wraps tight around the rim, then clipping it with a chip clip allowed normal bags to be used. But since this interferes with the company's raquet of selling bags I imagine this review will not be published for long.

In the end we decided that the trash bags that the company sells, which are designed specifically to fit this trash can, and which are of excellent quality, strength and thickness are a small indulgence that we can accept, so I bought a 20 box of them until I can come up with a better way to use normal bags again.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Excellent can, and excellent (but expensive) bags

Since then an auto closing trash can with a solid seal is a must. This has that.

The mouth is wide enough that normal trash bags (Glad, Hefty, the usual brands), stretch over it and provide a solid hold. Last trash can I had I had to use rubber bands or something to try to hold the top in place and it never worked quite well enough. Not a problem with this trash can.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I'm the first to literally laugh out loud with derision at the three figure price on this trash can, seriously. That's just beyond insane in our world. However, if you can justify the splurge on a garbage can, then this thing is pretty darn good. I have to admit it's the best garbage can we've ever had.

First of all, it's attractive to look at and it fits right in with our stainless appliances. The curved body gives it a very modern and sleek aesthetic that doesn't scream "Trash here!" The step is light and responsive and the lid rises immediately and silently. It self closes without banging down which I very much appreciate. I also like the fingerprint resistant external material - mainly because it also deters nose prints from our six dogs.

The inside is split between two containers needing two bags. If you're in an area that does separate recycling this is very handy. Just pull out the blue plastic bucket with one type of stuff and everything else is on the other side. For my needs, we just put a bag liner on each side and go about our business. The liners from SimpleHuman are expensive, fortunately, plain old Hefty 13 gallon trash bags and the 13 gallon generic trash bags from Kroger both fit just fine in this can. That means this is a dog resistant, smell resistant, regular bag using, good looking, fully functional, extra capacity trash can that makes no noise and is hands free - awesome!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Hands free, noiseless operation. Resists dog nose prints & generic 13 gallon trash bags fit fine

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Here's the 2 things I needed to know and had a hard time finding in reviews:
1) The large side most definitely fits a 13 gallon tall kitchen trash bag. You pull the excess thru the rubber clip (see photo), it stays with no issue until you're ready to take the trash out.
2) You can almost fill an entire 13 gallon bag full of trash in the large side (see photo). If you totally squash your trash down like a maniac, you probably could fill the whole bag. So you do NOT need to purchase weird size trash bags for the large side!!!!!
The smaller side will also hold a 13 gallon bag if you want to put one in there just to keep the can clean. You'll have some excess bag but again- just pull it thru the rubber clip.
The trash can is large- yes of course because it's actually 2 trash cans. But it's not monstrous as some have indicated. I have a small kitchen & it fits better than the 2 separate cans that I was previously using. Really happy with this one - just wish it was available in white when I purchased.
